etymology.description: 𣒚 (depicts a bag with a bottom) + 石 (phonetic) = a bag
etymology.interpretation: In its original form, 橐(橐) (tuó, 'bag') depicted a bag with a distinct bottom, emphasizing its function of holding items securely. Over time, 石(石) (shí, 'stone') was borrowed phonetically to provide the pronunciation, while maintaining the visual idea of a bag in combination with 𣒚, which represents the conceptual image of a bag.
